param.name: kmer_length
param.label: K-mer length
param.help: Length of the k-mers (or minimizers) to hash. Leave blank to use the maximum length expressible for the chosen alphabet (31 for DNA, 14 for protein). Values larger than that limit switch dashing2 to rolling hashing, allowing unbounded k.

param.name: sketch_size
param.label: Sketch size
param.help: Number of registers retained per sketch. Larger sketches give more accurate similarity estimates at the cost of memory and time. Unlike Dashing 1, any positive integer is allowed (it need not be a power of two).

param.label: Window size (winnowing)
param.help: When greater than the k-mer length, only the minimum-hash k-mer in each window of this size is kept (minimizer winnowing). Larger windows yield fewer minimizers and faster sketching. Leave blank to use every k-mer.

param.label: Spaced-seed pattern
param.help: Spacing scheme for spaced minimizers, given as one fewer integer than the k-mer length, e.g. for -k5 the value 0,1,1,0 specifies the match pattern KK.K.KK where '.' positions are ignored. Run-length form such as 0,1x2,0 is also accepted. Leave blank for contiguous (unspaced) k-mers.

validator.message: Use comma-separated integers, optionally run-length compressed (e.g. 0,1,1,0 or 0,1x2,0).

param.label: Disable canonicalization
param.help: By default DNA k-mers are canonicalized with their reverse complement so the sketch is strand-independent. Enable this to make sketching strand-specific. Ignored for protein alphabets.

param.label: Use 128-bit k-mers
param.help: Encode k-mers in 128-bit integers instead of 64-bit, allowing exact encoding of DNA k-mers up to length 64.

param.label: Hash seed
param.help: Seed for the k-mer hash function. A value of 0 disables k-mer XORing so k-mers are encoded directly. Leave blank to use the default seed.

param.name: alphabet
param.label: Sequence alphabet
param.help: Alphabet used to interpret the input sequences. DNA is canonicalized by default; any protein alphabet disables canonicalization. The reduced alphabets group chemically similar residues to improve sensitivity for divergent proteins.

param.name: sketch_mode
param.label: Sketching mode
param.help: Data structure used to summarize each input. One-permutation SetSketch is the fastest and treats inputs as sets. Full SetSketch is more accurate for large sketches and small sets. The exact modes (k-mer set / count dictionary) build sorted hash sets rather than sketches.
param.option: Full SetSketch—set-based, better for large sketches (--full)

param.name: measure
param.label: Comparison measure
param.help: Quantity emitted for each pair. Similarity is the fraction of shared registers. Mash/Poisson distance converts similarity to an evolutionary distance suitable for clustering. Containment is asymmetric (|A&B|/|A|); symmetric containment uses the smaller set. Intersection and union report raw cardinalities.

param.name: result_shape
param.label: Output shape
param.help: Dense all-pairs layout. Symmetric emits an upper-triangular PHYLIP matrix. Square emits a full N×N matrix (use with an asymmetric measure such as containment). For a query-vs-reference (rectangular) comparison, additionally supply a query collection above.

param.name: mode
param.label: Sparse output filter
param.help: Restrict the dense matrix to its most important entries, emitting a compressed-sparse-row (CSR) result powered by an LSH index. Top-k keeps each item's nearest neighbours; thresholding keeps pairs above a similarity cutoff.

param.label: Number of nearest neighbours (k)
param.help: Maximum number of nearest neighbours listed per item. If k is greater than N-1, full pairwise distances are emitted instead.

param.name: threshold
param.label: Minimum similarity
param.help: Only pairs whose similarity exceeds this fraction are emitted.

param.label: Binary output
param.help: Emit the distances as a binary float32 matrix rather than human-readable text. Symmetric comparisons emit a condensed matrix; square and rectangular comparisons emit full row-major matrices.
